name: product-design-orchestrator
description: Coordinate a product-design effort from uncertain request through evidence, design, implementation, verification, and live learning without applying unnecessary ceremony.

name: project-inspector
description: Inspect an existing product and repository before UI changes so design decisions extend the real system instead of inventing a parallel one.

name: skill-router
description: Select the smallest useful bundled or external skill set by decision, lifecycle phase, risk, platform, and specificity.

name: decision-facilitation
description: Turn design disagreement or ambiguity into explicit criteria, trade-offs, reversible decisions, and proportionate tests.

name: design-critique
description: Critique a product experience by intent, user outcome, structure, interaction, system, and craft, then prioritize actionable corrections.

name: product-discovery
description: Understand the problem space, users, current journey, constraints, and opportunity before committing to a production solution.

name: robust-research
description: Design and govern credible mixed-method product research with explicit evidence labels, bias controls, contradictions, ethics, and confidence.

name: desk-research
description: Conduct current, source-grounded desk research for product, market, policy, standards, patterns, and technology decisions.

name: research-planning
description: Create a proportionate research plan that connects decisions and assumptions to participants, methods, logistics, analysis, and ethics.

name: user-interviews
description: Plan, moderate, and analyze user interviews that reveal context, behavior, motivations, constraints, and mental models without pitching the solution.

name: contextual-inquiry
description: Observe people performing real work in context to uncover tacit behavior, environmental constraints, artifacts, interruptions, and workarounds.

name: survey-quant
description: Design surveys and quantitative studies that measure the right construct with honest sampling, analysis, and uncertainty.

name: research-synthesis
description: Turn real qualitative and quantitative evidence into themes, models, opportunities, and decisions while preserving contradictions and traceability.

name: inclusive-research
description: Plan and conduct research that includes disabled people and participants facing language, literacy, device, bandwidth, confidence, or access barriers.

name: usability-testing
description: Evaluate whether representative people can complete realistic tasks, understand system feedback, and recover from problems in an appropriate-fidelity prototype or product.

name: problem-framing
description: Turn a feature request or diffuse opportunity into an evidence-aware problem statement, scope, principles, and assumption map.

name: outcomes-metrics
description: Define user-centered success measures, product outcomes, quality and harm guardrails, instrumentation, and decision thresholds.

name: competitive-landscape
description: Analyze competitors, substitutes, conventions, and whitespace to inform strategy and design without copying surface aesthetics.

name: jobs-to-be-done
description: Frame progress people seek, triggers, forces, desired outcomes, and competing solutions without reducing users to demographics or features.

name: journey-service-blueprint
description: Map the end-to-end user journey and backstage service system across time, channels, actors, evidence, handoffs, failures, and opportunities.

name: experiment-design
description: Design ethical product experiments that test a causal hypothesis with valid metrics, guardrails, assignment, analysis, and stopping rules.

name: trust-privacy-safety
description: Design user agency, privacy, security communication, reversible control, and harm prevention into sensitive or high-consequence experiences.

name: information-architecture
description: Design coherent concepts, taxonomy, navigation, labeling, content models, and retrieval paths based on user mental models and growth needs.

name: user-flows
description: Model complete user and system flows with decisions, states, alternate paths, permissions, recovery, and cross-channel handoffs.

name: interaction-design
description: Define how controls, feedback, state transitions, direct manipulation, keyboard/touch input, and recovery behave over time.

name: content-design
description: Design interface language, hierarchy, help, errors, confirmations, and content models so people can understand and act.

name: forms-validation
description: Design forms, data entry, validation, review, submission, and recovery for clarity, efficiency, accessibility, and data integrity.

name: onboarding
description: Design first-use, setup, education, activation, and progressive learning around real user value rather than product tours.

name: search-filtering
description: Design search, query, autocomplete, filters, facets, sorting, result interpretation, and no-result recovery for realistic information spaces.

name: data-dense-interfaces
description: Design dashboards, tables, admin, monitoring, and operational tools for scanning, comparison, action, exception handling, and scale.

name: ai-experience
description: Design AI-assisted and generative product experiences with clear capability, provenance, uncertainty, control, review, recovery, and evaluation.

name: collaboration-permissions
description: Design multi-user roles, sharing, permissions, presence, comments, approvals, conflicts, history, and accountability.

name: creative-direction
description: Translate audience, product, brand, and strategic intent into a coherent visual thesis, creative tension, system grammar, and signature move.

name: design-lab
description: Explore and compare meaningful design variants through focused questions, controlled changes, rendered options, and iterative feedback.

name: reference-analysis
description: Analyze screenshots, products, brand systems, and visual references into transferable principles without copying their surface identity.

name: anti-slop-taste
description: Detect and remove generic AI-made visual patterns while preserving context, usability, brand, and deliberate familiar conventions.

name: typography
description: Design and implement a readable, expressive, responsive type system with intentional hierarchy, rhythm, content fit, and language support.

name: color
description: Build a perceptually coherent, accessible color system for brand, hierarchy, interaction, status, data, themes, and real display conditions.

name: layout-composition
description: Design responsive spatial systems, grids, hierarchy, rhythm, and optical balance from content and task rather than template geometry.

name: imagery-iconography
description: Direct photography, illustration, diagrams, texture, icons, and media as a coherent semantic and brand system with accessible alternatives.

name: motion
description: Design purposeful, interruptible, performant motion that explains continuity, causality, hierarchy, feedback, or spatial relationships and respects user preferences.

name: responsive-design
description: Design adaptive experiences across viewport, device, orientation, input, content, and platform constraints rather than treating mobile as a scaled desktop.

name: data-visualization
description: Select, design, annotate, and implement charts and visual explanations that support accurate comparison, trend, distribution, relationship, and decision making.

name: design-tokens
description: Define platform-agnostic primitive, semantic, and component design tokens with themes, aliases, descriptions, governance, and code translation.

name: component-system
description: Specify reusable components with purpose, anatomy, variants, states, accessibility, content, tokens, API boundaries, tests, and misuse guidance.

name: accessibility-by-design
description: Translate accessibility and inclusive user needs into flow, component, content, token, and acceptance requirements before implementation.

name: theming
description: Design semantic theme architecture for light/dark, high contrast, brand, density, and platform modes with predictable inheritance and asset behavior.

name: documentation-governance
description: Document, govern, evolve, and measure adoption of a design system through clear ownership, contribution, review, versioning, migration, and deprecation.

name: design-system-migration
description: Plan and execute migration from fragmented or legacy UI to a coherent system without breaking product behavior or freezing delivery.

name: stack-inspector
description: Detect a project’s platform, framework, versions, rendering, styling, state, data, component, test, and deployment conventions before UI implementation.

name: technology-adapter
description: Translate approved design behavior into the conventions and capabilities of any detected technology stack using current official documentation.

name: design-to-code
description: Translate approved flows, screen contracts, tokens, components, content, and states into maintainable production code in vertical slices.

name: web-ui-engineering
description: Implement semantic, responsive, accessible, performant web UI with robust browser behavior, intrinsic layout, and maintainable component boundaries.

name: native-ui-engineering
description: Implement faithful, accessible, performant native or cross-platform mobile/desktop UI using platform navigation, layout, input, lifecycle, and accessibility conventions.

name: design-system-integration
description: Integrate new UI into an existing design system by reusing primitives, adding justified tokens/components, and preventing local drift.

name: pixel-perfect-implementation
description: Recreate an approved visual reference with measured, maintainable, responsive implementation and a deterministic screenshot comparison loop.

name: i18n-content-resilience
description: Harden UI for localization, RTL, text expansion, locale formats, input methods, pluralization, and extreme/missing content.

name: performance
description: Design, implement, and verify UI performance for loading, responsiveness, visual stability, animation, memory, and realistic low-end conditions.

name: metadata-discoverability
description: Implement accurate page/app metadata, structured sharing, indexing controls, deep links, titles, and install/discovery surfaces without misleading users or crawlers.

name: visual-qa
description: Review rendered UI across approved references, viewports, states, themes, content extremes, and platforms; classify and correct visual/system defects.

name: browser-automation
description: Use browser automation to exercise journeys, capture deterministic screenshots, inspect accessibility and console/network behavior, and preserve reproducible QA evidence.

name: accessibility-audit
description: Audit a complete user journey against applicable accessibility standards using automated checks, manual inspection, keyboard, zoom/reflow, and assistive technology as warranted.

name: interaction-state-audit
description: Audit components and flows for complete, correct, accessible state transitions, feedback, concurrency, interruption, persistence, and recovery.

name: edge-case-hardening
description: Make a product production-ready across empty, slow, error, partial, stale, offline, permission, lifecycle, content, volume, and abuse edge cases.

name: test-strategy
description: Define a risk-based UI test strategy across unit, component, interaction, accessibility, visual, integration, end-to-end, performance, and exploratory testing.

name: release-readiness
description: Run a final cross-functional release gate covering user outcome, behavior, content, accessibility, visual fidelity, performance, resilience, privacy, observability, rollback, and ownership.

name: live-optimization
description: Improve a live product through outcome monitoring, field evidence, support/operational signals, research, experiments, and system updates.
